c74object.name: tap.diode~
c74object.description: The tap.diode~ object is a virtual-analog model of the 4-pole diode-ladder lowpass — the filter topology of the Roland TB-303, modeled from Tim Stinchcombe's published circuit analysis. Unlike the buffered transistor ladder (tap.ladder~), a diode ladder's stages load each other, spreading the poles far apart: the response has a shallow knee near cutoff that opens into a full 24 dB/octave slope (the honest version of the panel's famous "18 dB" claim), and the resonance is broad and squelchy rather than surgical. The frequency attribute is calibrated to the resonance peak. The resonance feedback path is high-passed (150 Hz, the hardware value): resonance thins as the cutoff comes down, the bass survives full squelch — and, exactly like a stock TB-303, the standard resonance range (up to 1) never quite self-oscillates. The range above 1 and the fbhp attribute are the documented circuit bends: extended resonance makes it sing at high cutoffs, and fbhp 0 converts the feedback to DC-coupled, giving exact self-oscillation tuning at any cutoff (with transistor-ladder-style bass droop as the trade). Saturating diode pairs bound everything; drive pushes the input into them. Runs oversampled (2x by default). A signal in the right inlet modulates the cutoff with true per-sample resolution. All parameters glide smoothly, and sixteen preset slots can be stored and morphed between. Single-channel: wrap in mc. for multichannel.

method.name: float
method.description: In the right inlet, sets the cutoff frequency in Hz.

method.name: store
method.description: Store the current parameters in a preset slot (1-16).

method.name: recall
method.description: Morph all parameters to a stored preset (1-16). The optional second argument overrides the morph time in milliseconds (default: the interp attribute).

method.name: clear
method.description: Reset the filter state (this also stops self-oscillation).

attribute.name: frequency
attribute.description: Cutoff frequency in Hz (10-20000), calibrated to the resonance peak / oscillation frequency. At low resonance the -3 dB point sits well below this — the diode ladder's spread poles, faithfully. A signal in the right inlet overrides this with per-sample resolution.

attribute.name: resonance
attribute.description: Resonance (0-1.5). 0-1 is the hardware-realistic range: squelchy, but — like a stock TB-303 — never quite self-oscillating. Above 1 is the documented bend: the filter sings at high cutoffs, bounded by the diodes. With fbhp 0 the threshold is exactly 1.0 and oscillation lands exactly on the cutoff.

attribute.name: drive
attribute.description: Input drive in dB (-24..24): how hard the signal hits the saturating diode pairs.

attribute.name: fbhp
attribute.description: Resonance-feedback high-pass corner in Hz (0-2000; default 150, the hardware value). This is why resonance thins toward the bass and why the low end survives full squelch. 0 bends the circuit to DC-coupled feedback: transistor-ladder behavior, exact oscillation tuning, low-end droop at high resonance.

attribute.name: solver
attribute.description: Nonlinear solver for the coupled diode equations: 0 = fast (secant-linearized solve plus one corrective pass, the default), 1 = exact (re-linearization iterated to convergence). Audibly different only when drive and resonance are both pushed hard.

attribute.name: oversample
attribute.description: Internal oversampling factor (1, 2, or 4; default 2). Higher factors reduce aliasing from the diode saturation, at higher CPU cost.

attribute.name: interp
attribute.description: Default preset-morph time in milliseconds used by the recall message.

attribute.name: smooth
attribute.description: Anti-zipper interpolation time in milliseconds applied to parameter changes.
